name: BitMEX Crypto Derivatives Exchange API
what_it_does: Cryptocurrency derivatives exchange REST and WebSocket API — enabling professional traders to access perpetual swaps, futures, and options on Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies, with HMAC-SHA256 authentication, real-time WebSocket streaming with delta-encoded order book updates, testnet environment, and official API connectors in multiple languages.
use_cases: Trading Bitcoin perpetual swaps via BitMEX REST API for leveraged directional strategies
Streaming real-time delta-encoded order book via BitMEX WebSocket for market-making bots
Hedging Bitcoin exposure via BitMEX futures API for portfolio risk management
Backtesting trading strategies with historical OHLCV data via BitMEX API
Building algorithmic liquidation management via BitMEX API for position sizing
not_for: US users (BitMEX legally restricted from serving US persons — significant legal history)
Retail traders without derivatives knowledge and risk management experience
best_when: A non-US professional trader needs a mature derivatives exchange with high leverage (up to 100x on BTC) and deep BitMEX perpetual swap liquidity for hedging or speculation.
avoid_when: You're a US person (legal risk), need spot trading, or require regulated derivatives exchange with regulatory oversight.

auth.notes: API Key + API Secret for HMAC-SHA256 signing. api-key header. api-signature header. api-expires header (Unix timestamp + 5-second expiry). Signature: HMAC-SHA256 of verb + path + expires + data. Testnet: testnet.bitmex.com (separate keys from mainnet). API key permissions: order, orderCancel, withdraw, margin, position, chat. No OAuth2. WebSocket: authenticate via auth message with same signature scheme.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; maker -0.01% (rebate) / taker 0.05% for perpetuals
pricing.notes: Seychelles/Hong Kong incorporated. Founded 2014 (Arthur Hayes, Ben Delo, Samuel Reed). Pioneered perpetual swap (Bitcoin perpetual). CFTC/DOJ settlement 2021: $100M fine for unlawfully serving US customers. Founded BitMEX Insurance Fund. Founders stepped down 2020 following indictment (later settled). Replaced leadership. 100x leverage on BTC perpetual (now reduced to 20x-100x by product). XBTUSD perpetual is most traded instrument. Delta-encoded WebSocket order book — must track incremental updates. Testnet.bitmex.com for development. OpenAPI spec via Swagger. 7 official SDK languages. Exchange continues operating under new management.

performance.rate_limits: 120 requests/minute for most endpoints; 30/minute for order creation

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HMAC-SHA256 with 5-second expires. Delta-encoded WebSocket order book. Testnet available. US persons legally restricted. $100M CFTC fine 2021. Seychelles. New management since 2020. Good OpenAPI spec/Swagger.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order reads idempotent by orderID. clOrdID (client order ID) for deduplication. api-expires header provides 5-second replay protection. Cancel idempotent by orderID.
known_agent_gotchas: US persons legally restricted — significant regulatory/legal risk for US users
api-expires: Unix timestamp 5-10 seconds in future — signature invalid after expiry
WebSocket order book is delta-encoded (partial table updates) — must track state locally
Testnet uses testnet.bitmex.com — create separate API keys (different from mainnet keys)
Order rate limit: 30/minute for order creation (lower than read endpoints)
$100M CFTC settlement 2021 for serving US customers — vendor risk consideration

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 with 5-second expiry (replay protection). API key permission scopes. Seychelles jurisdiction. US persons restricted (CFTC settlement 2021 — legal precedent). Founders indicted/settled DOJ charges. New management. Cryptocurrency derivatives — high leverage, high risk. Insurance fund for socialised losses. 100x leverage positions create systemic risk.
